description: Catalogo de controles de seguridad mapeados a categorias STRIDE con referencias OWASP, CWE y NIST

controls.control_standard: OWASP A07:2021 - Identification and Authentication Failures

implementation_hints.Python:
# django-axes — account lockout after N failures
# settings.py
INSTALLED_APPS += ['axes']
AUTHENTICATION_BACKENDS = ['axes.backends.AxesStandaloneBackend', 'django.contrib.auth.backends.ModelBackend']
AXES_FAILURE_LIMIT = 5
AXES_COOLOFF_TIME = 1  # hours

# Always pass parameters as second arg — never f-string or % format in SQL
cursor.execute(
    "SELECT id, username FROM users WHERE username = %s AND password = %s",
    (username, hashed_password)
)
# Django ORM is safe by default
User.objects.filter(username=username, password=hashed_password).first()

controls.control_name: Output Encoding and Content Security Policy

implementation_hints.Python:
# Django auto-escapes template variables — use mark_safe only for trusted content
from django.utils.html import escape
safe_output = escape(user_input)

# Add CSP header via django-csp
# settings.py
MIDDLEWARE += ['csp.middleware.CSPMiddleware']
CSP_DEFAULT_SRC = ("'self'",)
CSP_SCRIPT_SRC = ("'self'",)

controls.control_name: Structured Audit Logging with Actor Context

implementation_hints.Python:
import logging, json
audit_logger = logging.getLogger('audit')
handler = logging.FileHandler('audit.log')
handler.setFormatter(logging.Formatter('%(message)s'))
audit_logger.addHandler(handler)

def audit_log(actor, action, resource):
    audit_logger.info(json.dumps({'actor': actor, 'action': action, 'resource': resource, 'ts': str(datetime.utcnow())}))

controls.control_standard: OWASP A09:2021 - Security Logging and Monitoring Failures

implementation_hints.Python:
from cryptography.hazmat.primitives import hashes, serialization
from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric import ec
import json, base64

def sign_event(payload: dict, private_key) -> str:
    data = json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True).encode()
    signature = private_key.sign(data, ec.ECDSA(hashes.SHA256()))
    return base64.b64encode(signature).decode()

implementation_hints.Java:
// Spring Security — enable all recommended security headers
http.headers(headers -> headers
    .frameOptions(frame -> frame.deny())
    .xssProtection(xss -> xss.headerValue(XXssProtectionHeaderWriter.HeaderValue.ENABLED_MODE_BLOCK))
    .contentTypeOptions(Customizer.withDefaults())
    .httpStrictTransportSecurity(hsts -> hsts
        .includeSubDomains(true)
        .maxAgeInSeconds(31536000))
    .contentSecurityPolicy(csp -> csp.policyDirectives("default-src 'self'")));
implementation_hints.Python:
# Django settings.py — enable security headers
SECURE_BROWSER_XSS_FILTER = True
SECURE_CONTENT_TYPE_NOSNIFF = True
X_FRAME_OPTIONS = 'DENY'
SECURE_HSTS_SECONDS = 31536000
SECURE_HSTS_INCLUDE_SUBDOMAINS = True
# Add django-csp for Content-Security-Policy
CSP_DEFAULT_SRC = ("'self'",)

# Django — DEBUG=False in production + custom 500 view
# settings.py
DEBUG = False
ALLOWED_HOSTS = ['yourdomain.com']

# views.py
def handler500(request):
    import logging
    logging.getLogger('django.request').error('500 error', exc_info=True)
    return JsonResponse({'error': 'Internal server error'}, status=500)

controls.control_name: Sensitive Field Filtering in API Responses

implementation_hints.Python:
# Django REST Framework serializer — write_only for sensitive fields
class UserSerializer(serializers.ModelSerializer):
    password = serializers.CharField(write_only=True)
    class Meta:
        model = User
        fields = ['id', 'username', 'email', 'password']
        # password will only be accepted on write, never returned

DATA_UPLOAD_MAX_MEMORY_SIZE = 2621440  # 2.5MB
FILE_UPLOAD_MAX_MEMORY_SIZE = 5242880  # 5MB
DATA_UPLOAD_MAX_NUMBER_FIELDS = 1000

workers = 4

controls.control_name: Connection Pool and Circuit Breaker Configuration

controls.control_standard: OWASP A07:2021 - Identification and Authentication Failures

implementation_hints.Python:
# Django AUTH_PASSWORD_VALIDATORS in settings.py
AUTH_PASSWORD_VALIDATORS = [
    {'NAME': 'django.contrib.auth.password_validation.MinimumLengthValidator', 'OPTIONS': {'min_length': 12}},
    {'NAME': 'django.contrib.auth.password_validation.CommonPasswordValidator'},
    {'NAME': 'django.contrib.auth.password_validation.NumericPasswordValidator'},
    # Install django-password-validators for complexity
    {'NAME': 'password_validators.password_character_requirements.password_validation.PasswordCharacterValidator',
     'OPTIONS': {'min_length_digit': 1, 'min_length_upper': 1, 'min_length_special': 1}}
]
# Use Django's built-in bcrypt hasher
PASSWORD_HASHERS = ['django.contrib.auth.hashers.BCryptSHA256PasswordHasher']
